recentResults.venue: Maharaja Yadavindra Singh Stadium, New Chandigarh
recentResults.highlight: Priyansh Arya 93(37) & Connolly 87(46) smash 182-run stand — PBKS post season-high 254, stay unbeaten at 5-0
Tilak Varma 101*(45) maiden IPL ton — joint-fastest for MI; Ashwani Kumar 4/24, Bumrah strikes first ball as MI end 4-match slump
Abhishek Sharma 135*(68) — 9th T20 ton equals Kohli's Indian record; Eshan Malinga 4/32, SRH win 3rd on the trot, climb to 3rd
Jadeja 43*(29) rescues RR to 159; Jofra Archer 3/20, Nandre Burger 2/27 defend lowest IPL 2026 total — LSG's 4th straight loss, RR jump to 2nd
Sanju Samson 101*(54) — first El Clasico without Dhoni or Rohit; Akeal Hosein 4/17, Noor Ahmad 2/19, Ghazanfar 2/? — MI's BIGGEST defeat in IPL history (by runs), CSK climb to 5th

storylines.title: Samson's 101* hands MI their biggest-ever IPL defeat — CSK win first El Clasico without Dhoni or Rohit
storylines.body: Chennai Super Kings thumped Mumbai Indians by 103 runs at Wankhede on April 23 — MI's biggest-ever defeat in IPL history by runs. Sanju Samson's unbeaten 101 off 54 (10 fours, 6 sixes) was his second century of IPL 2026 and came in an otherwise anaemic CSK batting card — the second-highest score was just 22. Ruturaj Gaikwad (22) and Dewald Brevis (21) got starts but couldn't convert; Shivam Dube's poor run continued with 5. CSK posted 207/6. MI's chase never started: Akeal Hosein (4/17 in 4 overs) struck with his first and second overs as MI slumped to 11/3, then 29/3 in the powerplay — their lowest of IPL 2026. Noor Ahmad (2/19) and Ghazanfar (2/?) choked the middle; only Suryakumar (43) and Tilak Varma (37) resisted with a 73-run stand. MI bowled out for 104 in 19 overs. Historic in another way: first El Clasico without both MS Dhoni (knee issue) and Rohit Sharma (hamstring) in the XI. Nine wickets for spinners — the most in any IPL game at Wankhede.

storylines.title: Anshul Kamboj reclaims Purple Cap with 14 wickets — CSK's quiet bargain is IPL 2026's leading pacer
storylines.body: Just 24 hours after Prince Yadav snatched it from him, CSK's Anshul Kamboj is back at the top of the Purple Cap list with 14 wickets in 7 matches at an economy of 8.95 and average of 15.78. He picked up two against MI — his 12th and 13th dismissal of the season came in consecutive balls during the collapse. Prince Yadav (LSG, 13), Eshan Malinga (SRH, 12), Prasidh Krishna (GT, 12), and Jofra Archer (RR, 11) round out the top 5 — five teams, five wicket-takers, all within 3 wickets. The tightest Purple Cap race in recent IPL memory.

storylines.title: Samson enters Orange Cap top 3 — 293 runs at SR 178, two centuries in IPL 2026
storylines.body: Sanju Samson's El Clasico ton vaulted him from outside the top 5 into the Orange Cap top 3 with 293 runs at an average of 58.60 and SR of 178.65. He's now the only player in IPL 2026 with two centuries (115* vs DC, 101* vs MI) — and his numbers look better than anyone else's when innings are weighted by match situation. SRH openers Abhishek Sharma (323) and Heinrich Klaasen (320) still hold the top two spots, while Shubman Gill (265) and Vaibhav Sooryavanshi (254) drop a place each. With CSK suddenly back in the playoff conversation at 5th, Samson's form is the biggest shift in the tournament.

storylines.title: A rivalry redefined — the first MI vs CSK without Dhoni or Rohit, and CSK didn't need them
storylines.body: For the first time in IPL history, both MS Dhoni (knee issue) and Rohit Sharma (hamstring) were absent from an El Clasico playing XI. Dhoni kept wickets and batted in the nets earlier in the week but hasn't played a match this season. Rohit had been training but missed a session before the game. Instead, two other captains — Ruturaj Gaikwad and Hardik Pandya — and a new CSK talisman in Sanju Samson wrote the script. That CSK dominated without their legend, and MI collapsed without theirs, may become the defining moment of the transition era for both franchises. With MI now 2-5 and on -0.736 NRR, Hardik Pandya's playoff math looks grim.

storylines.title: RR defend lowest total of IPL 2026 — Archer and Jadeja drag Royals back to 2nd
storylines.body: Rajasthan Royals ended a two-match losing streak at Ekana on April 22 in unusual fashion — defending 159, the lowest total of IPL 2026 so far. Ravindra Jadeja (43* off 29) and impact sub Shubham Dubey stitched an unbeaten 49-run stand to push the total to 159/6 from 54/4. Chasing 160, LSG were routed: Jofra Archer (3/20) bowled a maiden over to Nicholas Pooran in the powerplay and returned to flatten Mohammed Shami's leg stump with a late-tailing yorker. Mitchell Marsh's 55 off 41 was the lone resistance; Rishabh Pant was run out for a duck. LSG folded for 119 in 18 overs — 4th straight loss. RR climb to 2nd with 10 points.

storylines.title: Pooran, Parag and Pant — three high-profile batters whose form has collapsed
storylines.body: Three of the most talked-about batters of IPL 2026 are collectively scraping bottom. Nicholas Pooran has 51 runs in 7 innings at the lowest strike rate in the tournament among batters facing 50+ balls. Rishabh Pant, LSG's Rs.27 Cr captain, was run out backing up too far for a duck against RR — his latest in a string of low scores drawing brutal fan reaction. Riyan Parag has 61 runs in 7 innings as RR captain with a highest of 20. That all three play for teams in trouble (LSG 9th, RR just recovered to 2nd, MI 8th) is no coincidence. Whether any of them figure out form before the playoff race closes will decide three season narratives.

South Africa opener — 112*(60) vs PBKS was his peak; golden duck vs CSK continues MI's batting slump
